steps.title: Distribute the multiplier
steps.content:
Multiply $17$ by each term inside the parentheses. The first term becomes $17x^2$, and the constant term comes from multiplying $17$ by $-6$:
$$17(x^2-6)=17x^2-17\cdot 6$$

steps.title: Simplify the constant term
steps.content:
Compute the product:
$$17\cdot 6=102$$So the equivalent expression is
$$17x^2-102$$This expression has the same variable term as the original expression and the simplified constant term. The subtraction inside the parentheses becomes subtraction of $102$ after distribution. The answer is D.
